name: Benavente: Tera and Esla River Valleys Loop
name_original: Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente
description: This moderate 42.6-mile road cycling route, Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente, offers pleasant scenery around Benavente
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Benavente, located in the province of Zamora, Spain, offer diverse terrain across the fields of Zamora. The region is characterized by open landscapes, including routes that pass by the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons and along the Río Esla. Road cyclists can expect mostly well-paved surfaces with varying elevation gains, providing options for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Benavente The most popular road cycling route is Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente, a 42.6 miles (68.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the open fields surrounding Benavente. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Benavente – tour around Fuentes de Ropel and río Esla – Villafer, a moderate 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This route follows the Esla River, offering scenic views of the diverse terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Benavente – tour around the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons, a 63.7 miles (102.5 km) trail leading through the natural reserve lagoons, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Benavente is defined by open fields, natural reserve lagoons, and river valleys, with options ranging from easy and moderate routes to more challenging paths. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Benavente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 6 reviews. More than 280 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Benavente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Benavente?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Benavente, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with more than 280 road cyclists using komoot to discover the area.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Benavente?
A: Road cycling in Benavente, located in the fields of Zamora, offers diverse open landscapes. You can expect routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023215007/benavente-tour-around-the-villafafila-and-villalpando-natural-reserve-lagoons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique natural views, and routes that follow the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025817800/benavente-tour-around-fuentes-de-ropel-and-rio-esla-villafer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla&lt;/a&gt;, providing scenic river valley experiences.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Benavente?
A: Yes, Benavente offers several routes suitable for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride. There are 5 easy routes available, characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces and varying elevation gains, making them ideal for a comfortable outing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Benavente?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Benavente are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351326158/mozar-y-santovenia-del-esla-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the open fields surrounding the town.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Benavente?
A: The region's open landscapes are generally pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite warm in the fields of Zamora, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if cycling during this season. Winter conditions should be checked for potential cold or wet weather.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Benavente has historical significance with monumental buildings that can be interesting stops. While cycling, you might pass by or near attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt; or the fortress Castillo de la Mota. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with nature observation in Benavente?
A: Definitely. The routes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023215007/benavente-tour-around-the-villafafila-and-villalpando-natural-reserve-lagoons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; are excellent for nature observation, particularly birdwatching. The Río Esla routes also offer opportunities to enjoy the local flora and fauna in a serene setting.

Q: Are there routes suitable for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: For advanced road cyclists, there is at least one difficult route available, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351275421/tabara-sarracin-de-aliste-ferreras-de-arriba-morales-del-rey-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tábara, Sarracín de Aliste, Ferreras de Arriba, Morales del Rey — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers a significant distance and elevation gain, requiring very good fitness. It may include some unpaved or more challenging segments.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in Benavente?
A: The duration of road cycling routes in Benavente varies widely. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351326158/mozar-y-santovenia-del-esla-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt; can take around 2 hours 50 minutes, while longer routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023215007/benavente-tour-around-the-villafafila-and-villalpando-natural-reserve-lagoons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente – tour around the Villafáfila and Villalpando Natural Reserve Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; might take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Benavente?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from open fields to natural reserve lagoons and river valleys, and the variety of routes that cater to different ability levels, ensuring a rewarding experience for all.

Q: Are there any specific bridges or river crossings of interest on the routes?
A: Yes, several routes in the Benavente area feature interesting bridges. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which can add historical and scenic value to your ride.

Q: Are there routes that explore the areas of Pobladura del Valle and Morales de Rey?
A: Yes, there is a dedicated route that takes you through these areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023203026/benavente-tour-around-pobladura-del-valle-and-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente – tour around Pobladura del Valle and Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 76 km path, offering a pleasant ride through these parts of the region.
tips: Mózar is a small town located at the confluence of the Tera and Órbigo rivers in the Benavente valley. Famous for its natural activities, there are several campsites near the river beaches for bathing and abundant places for trout fishing.

It was built in 1920 on the waters of the Esla river and joins the Zamora regions of Tierra de Campos with Tábara. Its architectural structure is solid, made of masonry stone and has nine semicircular arches.
Beautiful landscape of agricultural fields right next to Lagunas de Villafáfila Natural Park.
Metal statue of Don Quixote, a character from an epic novel by the famous Spanish writer Miguel de Cervantes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route, Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente, typically takes around 2 hours and 51 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 68.5-kilometer loop.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and is best suited for cyclists with a good fitness level. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces and doesn't have significant steep climbs, its distance of 68.5 kilometers requires some endurance. It's not ideal for absolute beginners but is manageable for those with some road cycling experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain along this route is predominantly well-paved, making for a generally smooth and easy ride. You won't encounter significant steep climbs, which is typical for a moderate road cycling trail.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific scenic details are not extensively documented, this route is recognized as one of the 'Top 10 Most Beautiful Road Biking Routes around Benavente.' You can expect pleasant surroundings typical of the Benavente area, including open fields and river views.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are a few notable points of interest. You'll pass by the Mózar River Beach on the Tera River, the historic Quintos Bridge Over the Esla River, and a Landscape of fields offering scenic views. The route also starts and ends near the Statue of Don Quixote – Benavente.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for a longer ride. Summer can be quite hot in this region, while winter might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Benavente, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, but public parking options in Benavente would serve as a convenient starting point.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog along for a 68.5-kilometer road bike ride is not typically recommended or practical. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can safely and comfortably accompany you for the entire duration, perhaps in a specialized bike trailer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Mózar y Santovenia del Esla — circular desde Benavente route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with several other paths. You'll find it crosses parts of the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and Camino Sanabrés, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or amenities along the way?
faqs.answer: Since the route starts and ends in Benavente, you'll have access to various amenities, cafes, and restaurants there. For stops along the 68.5-kilometer loop, it's advisable to carry your own water and snacks, as specific amenities directly on the route are not detailed. However, passing through villages like Mózar and Santovenia del Esla may offer opportunities for refreshments.
